share_basis_note: REFRESH of 2026-06-20. 24,391M diluted shares (Q1 FY27 weighted) x $210.96 (2026-07-10 close, last <= as_of) = ~$5.145T. Price from Yahoo chart API re-fetch [S106], cross-checked vs stockanalysis [S108] (both $210.96) per freshness.json. Price essentially flat vs 06-20 ($210.69, +0.13%) -> valuation conclusion UNCHANGED. FILED financials (Q1 FY27 / FY26) carried forward unchanged.

robotics_thesis.robotics_revenue_pct: <1% (~0.3-0.5%, UNDISCLOSED). NVDA does not break out robotics separately; it is a subset of the Automotive market platform (FY26 $2.35B = ~1.09% of revenue [S101]), which is itself mostly autonomous-vehicle DRIVE design-wins, not robots. Data Center (AI compute) = ~92% [S102]. Buying NVDA is an AI-datacenter compute bet, NOT robotics alpha.
robotics_thesis.demand_real_vs_anticipation: MOSTLY ANTICIPATION for robotics. Real-now cash in the segment = auto DRIVE ($2.35B FY26, +39% [S101]) -- but that is autos, not robots, and ~1%. Robotics itself = dev-kit/module hardware (Jetson Thor/T4000 $1,999/unit, IGX Thor) + Isaac/GR00T/Cosmos platform + named ecosystem (Boston Dynamics, Caterpillar, Franka, LG, NEURA) [S103] -- dated PRODUCT/ecosystem events, NOT dated robotics revenue. Humanoid ramp is option value, not cash.

capital_cycle.supply_barrier: high (Jetson compute + CUDA/Isaac/Omniverse software stack) BUT segment earns ~no excess return yet
capital_cycle.read: Robotics segment is too small and too early to show a supply-responsive excess-return imbalance for capital to compete away -- the capital-cycle mechanism is basically N/A here (no high return to attract supply). The lens's real object for NVDA is the DATA CENTER/GPU segment (partial / late? / CUDA-high vs raw-compute-low), unchanged from 06-20.
capital_cycle.caution: NONE material for robotics (not late/peak -- it is unmonetized option, not a top). Does not add or subtract size.

robotics_thesis.real_bottleneck_vs_concept: Real moat at the PLATFORM/developer level (Jetson + CUDA + Isaac/GR00T switching cost, falsifiable) -- NOT pure concept. BUT at current revenue scale it is still mostly CONCEPT/OPTION, not a monetized bottleneck. The proven, cash-generating bottleneck is AI-datacenter compute, not robotics.
robotics_thesis.option_vs_core_split: At $210.96 / $5.145T, ~100% of the market cap = the AI-datacenter compute business (~92% rev, ~28x fwd). The robotics/embodied-AI piece is a near-free embedded call: ~nil current cash, not separately valued. You pay NO robotics premium and get NO robotics alpha as a buyer. Conversely, for the batch's 'auto/industrial/robotics core' framing, that is only ~1% of NVDA -> buying NVDA for robotics exposure is 99% buying AI compute (wrong vehicle for pure robotics; see TER/ON/NOVT).

module_signals.finding: Anchor quarter (Q1 FY27) + FY26 baseline fully primary-sourced and carried forward unchanged (revenue $81.6B, DC $75.2B +92%, GM 74.9%, FCF $48.6B, Q2 guide $91B). LIVE price re-fetched 2026-07-10 = $210.96 and cross-checked vs 2 independent sources (freshness.json). Added robotics segment sourcing (Automotive FY26 $2.35B [S101], market-platform table [S102], products [S103]). Gaps (custom-silicon quant share, standalone robotics revenue, 10-K, proxy) are haircuts.

Full-stack accelerated compute (GPU + CUDA + NVLink/InfiniBand/Spectrum-X + systems) is the bottleneck the AI industry routes through. Demand is a multi-quarter primary-sourced forced update: DC $75.2B (+92%), networking $14.8B (+199%), Q2 guide $91B. exceptional_bottleneck. Robotics is NOT the mechanism being bought -- it is <1% of revenue and mostly anticipation (robotics_lens.md).

Captures the largest AI-compute profit pool via CUDA lock-in + full-stack + networking (+199%). Operator grade 4/5 (30y founder Huang, one-share-one-vote, capex-light). $80B buyback + dividend $0.01->$0.25 = real return but executed near-high (-10.8% off ATH), NOT cheap. Durability HIGH on training, MED on inference (custom-silicon battleground). Robotics platform moat is real but unmonetized. Cyclical -> size capped below Confirmed.

Owner earnings unusually clean: capex-light (FY26 $6.1B), FCF=OE run-rate ~$182B, net cash ~$72B (fortress). GAAP net income inflated ~$30.2B by marketable-equity gains -> use operating owner earnings. Bridge CLOSES; uncertainty is run-rate durability + entry price, not the bridge. Unchanged from 06-20 (FILED).

No structural breakage: thesis durability intact (bottleneck real, +92%/+199%), balance sheet survives the bear (net cash $72B). pull-forward + custom-silicon = SIZE control + kill monitors, not a hard veto. China = recoverable optionality (H200 license reinstated 2026-02, orders 2026-03), Q2 guide conservatively excludes it. Robotics adds NO downside (unmonetized option). Capital-cycle caution on robotics = NONE (not a top).

At $210.96 ($5.145T), forward P/E ~28x, starting OE yield ~3.5%, base 5y IRR +4.7% < 8% hurdle -> PRICE IS THE BINDING CONSTRAINT (no margin of safety). Stock -10.8% off ATH ($236.54), +30.5% off 52wk low ($161.61) -- upper part of range. Asymmetry does not favor a starter: bull +18.6% / base +4.7% / bear -17.6%. 8%-hurdle-neutral entry ~$181. Robotics option adds ~nil to valuation. Price flat vs 06-20 -> verdict unchanged.

starter_zone: No new money at $210.96. A structured STARTER (~3-5%) opens on a pullback into ~$155-181, where base 5y IRR climbs back above the 8% hurdle on an unchanged thesis.
add_zone: Scale toward 8-12% only into ~$142-155 (base IRR ~12-14%) AND/OR on confirmation (Q2/Q3 FY27 revenue meeting the $91B guide + DC margin/share holding). Cyclicality caps the ceiling below Core.
trim_or_no_chase_zone: Current $210.96 is ABOVE the ~$181 no-margin-of-safety line -> no chase; hard no-chase toward the $236.54 ATH. Existing holders HOLD an exceptional compounder; do not add new money here, do not trim (business unimpaired, no kill criterion fired).
binding_constraint: PRICE (no MOS). At $210.96 base 5y IRR +4.7% < 8% hurdle and the stock is only -10.8% off its ATH. The exceptional AI-compute bottleneck is intact and the owner-earnings bridge is clean, but valuation is the go/no-go. Robotics does NOT change this: it is <1% of revenue and mostly anticipation, adding no buyable increment at this price. Completeness ~65% independently caps the ceiling at STARTER; price gate binds harder -> WATCH.
kill_criteria: Revenue materially misses the $91B Q2 guide, OR DC revenue prints sequential declines, OR gross margin breaks below ~65%.
Credible evidence of large-scale CSP/custom-silicon design wins structurally eroding DC share (the ONLY escalate-to-veto path).
Large inventory / purchase-commitment write-down (FY26 had a $4.5B H20 precedent).
Price stays above ~$181 with no offsetting estimate raise -> remains WATCH / no-chase (already the case at $210.96).
Huang key-man / succession shock -> lower durability ceiling.
watch_to_starter_trigger: Price pullback into ~$155-181 (base 5y IRR back >= 8% hurdle) on an UNCHANGED thesis. Robotics-specific trigger to re-rate the option: NVDA breaks out robotics/embodied-AI as a standalone revenue line with sustained +xxx% Y/Y (concept -> monetized bottleneck) -- until then it stays a free call, not a buy reason.
runner_dissent: Same as 06-20, reaffirmed. Bull instinct: exceptional cleanly-financed bottleneck (FCF=OE ~$182B, net cash $72B, +92% DC), bull 5y IRR +18.6% -- why not a starter? Framework overrides to WATCH because PRICE is the constraint: base 5y IRR +4.7% < 8% hurdle, stock -10.8% off ATH, and a single $91B guide cannot underwrite paying up for a possibly cycle-peak run-rate. On the batch thesis specifically: the robotics narrative is a genuine platform position but NOT a buyable robotics bottleneck at NVDA -- it is <1% of revenue, mostly anticipation, and contributes ~nil to the price. Buying NVDA here is an AI-compute valuation call with no MOS, plus a free robotics lottery ticket -- not robotics alpha. OPEN: custom-silicon quantitative share (O4, blocking), standalone robotics revenue (O7), humanoid ramp magnitude (O8), 10-K (O1), proxy (O3), China H200 magnitude (O5).
